title: Offer Evaluation, Compensation, Negotiation, and Decision Quality · Hiring Signal Forensics
summary: A realistic offer evaluation, compensation, negotiation, and decision quality artifact contains one plausible targeting, evidence, integrity, access, privacy, process, or decision defect. Locate the first unsupported claim before revising it. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in evidence-based offer decision model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.
Compare base, variable pay, equity uncertainty, benefits, leave, hours, commute, equipment, learning, stability, risk, and personal constraints.
Build a source-dated occupation, location, level, and responsibility range while separating public data from this employer decision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Written Offer
steps.instruction:
debug case — A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The debug variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.

Inspect the Written Offer trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Written Offer.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Written Offer, keep Written Offer connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Written Offer, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Written Offer, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Written Offer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Written Offer, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The debug variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Written Offer, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A verbal promise is equivalent to an enforceable final agreement.

stimulus.title: Written Offer case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The debug variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A verbal promise is equivalent to an enforceable final agreement.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date. Artifact under review: evidence-based offer decision model.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Written Offer.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Written Offer, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The debug variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint.
